instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Why should NRIs Prefer Buying Term Insurance in India Instead of Abroad?
Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) are Indians working abroad. Though NRIs live in foreign countries, they may prefer buying financial products in India. Buying term insurance in India is a common example.
Many NRIs prefer buying term insurance in India1 to provide financial stability for their families. Even data suggests the same.
What is the reason for such preference? Why do NRIs buy term insurance in India? Let’s understand.
Reasons NRIs buy term insurance in India
To enjoy affordable premiums
The premiums for term insurance in India tend to be affordable than those in foreign countries, depending on where the person may be residing1. According to the MD and CEO of Bajaj Allianz Life, Mr. Tarun Chugh, average overseas premiums are 20% higher as compared to that in India1.
This means that, for the same coverage, NRIs have to shell out a higher premium in their country of residence than in India.
For debt repayments
If NRIs have availed of a loan in India, a term plan helps in repayment of the debt in their absence. NRIs may opt for mortgage redemption plans which are decreasing term plans tied to the loan. In the case of premature demise, the term plan may help in paying off the outstanding balance of the loan. This may avoid the debt burden from falling on the family.
Because of the simple process
Buying a term plan in India is a simple affair. NRIs can buy the policy online in simple steps. Alternatively, they can buy the policy on their trip to India. They may have to undergo a tele medical or physical health screening while opting for the policy2. Generally, the costs for the medical tests done outside India, may have to be borne by the individual, depending upon the terms and conditions laid down by the insurer.
Moreover, online term insurance calculators help NRIs find the right coverage so that they can financially secure their families adequately.
For the inherent tax benefits
NRIs are eligible for tax benefits on the premiums paid under Section 80C and on the maturity amount as per Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to satisfaction of the terms and conditions stated therein. Thus, if NRIs have taxable income in India, they can reduce their tax liability besides enjoying insurance coverage.
Thus, given these benefits, NRIs prefer to buy a term insurance plan in India. The plan gives them the coverage that they need at affordable premiums and helps them secure their family’s financial future in their absence.
If you are an NRI, consider investing in a comprehensive term plan. Use the term insurance calculator and figure out the right coverage. You may opt for coverage to take care of your family even when you are not around.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. 2https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/nri/invest/everything-you-need-to-know-as-an-nri-buying-term-insurance-policy-abroad/articleshow/86806503.cms
